name: Lybiidae
description: Lybiidae comprises the African barbets, a diverse family of colourful, stout-billed birds found across sub-Saharan Africa. They are primarily frugivorous and nest in tree cavities excavated with their powerful bills.

children.name: Trachyphonus
children.description: Large, strikingly patterned African barbets with bold spotted and barred plumage and a distinctive crest. They are more terrestrial than other barbets, often foraging on the ground for insects and fallen fruit.

children.name: Trachyphonus vaillantii
children.description: A familiar barbet of southern and East African savannas and woodlands, with striking black, white, yellow, and red plumage and a spiky black crest.

children.name: Trachyphonus erythrocephalus
children.description: A brilliantly coloured barbet of East Africa, the male has a vivid red head and breast, yellow belly, and black-and-white spotted upperparts.

children.name: Trachyphonus purpuratus
children.description: A large, forest-dwelling barbet of Central and West Africa, with a bright yellow bill, black-and-yellow spotted plumage, and a red rump.

children.name: Trachyphonus margaritatus
children.description: A barbet of the Sahel region from Mauritania to Sudan, with a bright yellow breast spotted with black, and a black crown. It inhabits dry savanna and acacia scrub.

children.name: Trachyphonus darnaudii
children.description: A small, social barbet of East African savannas and thorn scrub, with yellow-spotted black upperparts and a yellow breast with a black band. Named after the French explorer Joseph D'Arnaud.

children.name: Smilorbis afer
children.description: This species is found in the moist forests of eastern and central Africa, where it feeds on fruits and insects. It has a distinctive call that can be heard throughout its habitat.

children.name: Buccanodon phebus
children.description: These birds are found in the dense, humid forests of West Africa. They have vibrant plumage and a complex vocal repertoire that includes both calls and songs.

children.name: Gymnobucco lornis
children.description: Found in the mountainous regions of central Africa, these birds are known for their melodious songs and specialized diet that includes small mammals.

children.name: Pogoniulus bokii
children.description: These birds are common in the savannas and woodlands of southern Africa, where they primarily feed on fruits but also consume insects.

children.name: Stactolaema gilberti
children.description: Living in the rainforests of eastern Africa, these birds are characterized by their bright red face and vocal displays that can be heard over great distances.

children.name: Trachyphonus zephyrus
children.description: These birds are found in the high-altitude regions of East Africa, where they specialize in a diet of nectar and insects. Their flight is swift and direct.

children.name: Poagroenas dantei
children.description: These birds are primarily found in the coastal regions of East Africa, where they feed on insects and small reptiles. Their song is often heard during the breeding season.

children.name: Tricholaema thomasii
children.description: Living in the semi-arid regions of South Africa, these birds are known for their unique vocalizations and a diet that includes both insects and fruit.

children.name: Lybius kivuensis
children.description: These birds are found in the rainforests of Central Africa, where they feed on fruits and small vertebrates. They have a distinctive feeding habit that includes cracking open insects with their beaks.

children.name: Pogoniulus ruber
children.description: These birds are found in the coastal regions of East Africa, where they specialize in feeding on nectar and small insects. They have a distinctive reddish plumage.

children.name: Trachyphonus ruber
children.description: These birds are found in the coastal regions of East Africa, where they specialize in feeding on insects and small vertebrates. They have a distinctive reddish plumage.

children.name: Buccanodon zephyrus
children.description: These birds are found in the coastal regions of East Africa, where they specialize in feeding on fruits and insects. They have a distinctively loud call that echoes through their habitat.

children.name: Poagroenas zephyrus
children.description: These birds are found in the coastal regions of East Africa, where they specialize in feeding on fruits and insects. They have a distinctively loud call that echoes through their habitat.

children.name: Pogoniulus
children.description: Tiny barbets known as tinkerbirds, named for their repetitive, bell-like calls. They are among the smallest of all barbets, with a plump, compact body and short bill.

children.name: Pogoniulus chrysoconus
children.description: A widespread tinkerbird found across sub-Saharan Africa in savanna and woodland, with a distinctive yellow forehead patch and a penetrating 'tink' call.

children.name: Pogoniulus atroflavus
children.description: A small tinkerbird of West and Central African rainforest, with a striking red rump, black upperparts streaked with yellow, and a pale yellow belly.

children.name: Pogoniulus subsulphureus
children.description: A tiny tinkerbird of lowland rainforest from West Africa to Uganda, with a yellow throat and breast, olive upperparts, and a distinctive rapid trilling call.

children.name: Pogoniulus bilineatus
children.description: A common tinkerbird of forest and woodland across much of sub-Saharan Africa, with a bright yellow rump, black-and-white striped head, and a persistent piping call.

children.name: Pogoniulus makawai
children.description: A little-known tinkerbird known only from a single specimen collected in Zambia. Its white breast and plain olive upperparts distinguish it from other tinkerbirds.

children.name: Pogoniulus pusillus
children.description: A small tinkerbird of East and Southern African savannas, with a bright red forecrown, yellow rump, and a sharp, repetitive 'tink-tink-tink' call.

children.name: Pogoniulus leucomystax
children.description: A tinkerbird of East African montane forests, with a distinctive white moustachial stripe and yellow rump. Its call is a series of hollow 'pop' notes.

children.name: Pogoniulus coryphaea
children.description: A tinkerbird of West African montane forests from Guinea to Cameroon, with a grey crown and nape contrasting with bright yellow underparts.

children.name: Lybius
children.description: Medium-sized barbets with robust bills and colourful head and neck patterns. They are typically found in savanna and scrub habitats across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Lybius torquatus
children.description: A common barbet of southern and East Africa, with a red head and breast, grey-brown back, and a distinctive black collar. Its loud, duetting call is a familiar sound in gardens and woodlands.

children.name: Lybius leucocephalus
children.description: Found across Central and East Africa from Nigeria to Tanzania, with a white head, throat, and breast contrasting with a black back, wings, and tail.

children.name: Lybius dubius
children.description: A large, striking barbet of West African savannas, with a red head and breast, black back, and distinctive bristles at the base of the bill that give it the 'bearded' appearance.

children.name: Lybius guifsobalito
children.description: A barbet of East African woodlands from Ethiopia to Tanzania, with a red head and throat, black back, and a distinctive all-black bill.

children.name: Lybius rolleti
children.description: A barbet of the Sahel and Sudan savanna zones from Chad to Sudan and Uganda, with a white head, black breast band, and red lower belly.

children.name: Lybius melanopterus
children.description: A barbet of East African coastal forests and woodlands from Somalia to Mozambique, with a brown breast, white belly, and a black-and-white striped head.

children.name: Lybius minor
children.description: A small barbet of Central African savannas from Cameroon to Angola and the DRC, with a white face, black back, and red breast patch.

children.name: Lybius rubrifacies
children.description: A barbet restricted to a small range in Rwanda, Burundi, and neighbouring Tanzania. It has a vivid red face and throat with a black crown and is classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Lybius chaplini
children.description: A barbet endemic to south-central Zambia, with a white head and breast, a broad black collar, and a red belly. It is classified as Vulnerable due to its restricted range.

children.name: Tricholaema
children.description: A genus of medium-sized barbets with distinctive head patterning, typically sporting bold black-and-white facial stripes and colourful forehead patches.

children.name: Tricholaema leucomelas
children.description: A familiar barbet of southern African savannas and gardens, with a boldly patterned black-and-white head, red forehead, and a black band across the yellow breast.

children.name: Tricholaema lacrymosa
children.description: A barbet of East African highland forests, with a red forehead, yellow-spotted black head, and white underparts with black spotting on the flanks.

children.name: Tricholaema melanocephala
children.description: A barbet of dry savannas and acacia scrub in East Africa, with a black throat and crown, white eyebrow, and red forehead patch.

children.name: Tricholaema frontata
children.description: A barbet of the miombo woodlands of south-central Africa, with a red forehead, black-and-white head pattern, and a yellow belly. It is named for its association with Brachystegia woodland.

children.name: Tricholaema hirsuta
children.description: A forest barbet of West and Central Africa, with a black-and-white streaked throat, red forehead, and fine hair-like feathers on the breast.

children.name: Tricholaema diademata
children.description: A barbet of East African highlands from Ethiopia to Tanzania, with a bright red forehead patch, black-and-white head stripes, and a white belly with black spots.

children.name: Buccanodon
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Yellow-spotted Barbet, a distinctive West and Central African forest barbet.

children.name: Buccanodon duchaillui
children.description: A striking forest barbet of West and Central Africa, with a red head, black upperparts boldly spotted with yellow, and a yellow belly. It is named after the French-American explorer Paul du Chaillu.

children.name: Gymnobucco
children.description: A genus of mainly brown, partly bald barbets found in African forests. They are highly social, nesting colonially in dead trees.

children.name: Gymnobucco calvus
children.description: A brown barbet with a bare, dark-grey face and throat, found in lowland forests of West and Central Africa. It nests colonially in dead trees.

children.name: Gymnobucco peliocephalum
children.description: A small, brown barbet with a pale grey throat and a bristly forehead, found in forests from Guinea to the DRC.

children.name: Gymnobucco sladeni
children.description: A little-known barbet of Central African forests, with a brown body, bare facial skin, and a pale throat. It is sometimes considered a subspecies of G. calvus.

children.name: Gymnobucco bonapartei
children.description: A forest barbet of the Congo basin, with a grey throat, brown body, and bristly feathers around the base of the bill.

children.name: Stactolaema
children.description: A genus of small, primarily green-and-yellow barbets of African forests and woodlands. They have relatively plain plumage compared to other barbets.

children.name: Stactolaema leucotis
children.description: A barbet of East African coastal forests and the Eastern Arc Mountains, with a distinctive white ear patch, olive-green body, and yellow throat.

children.name: Stactolaema anchietae
children.description: A green-and-yellow barbet of Angolan and Zambian miombo woodlands, named after the Portuguese naturalist José de Anchieta. It has a yellow throat and a pale eye.

children.name: Stactolaema whytii
children.description: A barbet of miombo woodlands in south-central Africa, with a green body, yellow throat and breast, and a whitish eye. Named after the Scottish naturalist Alexander Whyte.

children.name: Stactolaema olivacea
children.description: A dull olive-green barbet of East African coastal and montane forests, with a pale yellow throat and a dark bill. It is inconspicuous and easily overlooked.

children.name: Smilorbis
children.description: A small genus of African barbets with distinctive facial markings.

children.name: Smilorbis leucocephalus
children.description: A barbet of East African savannas with a white head and black body.

children.name: Stactolaema olivacea
children.description: A dull olive-green barbet of East African coastal and montane forests.

children.name: Stactolaema virenzii
children.description: A recently described barbet of Central African forests, split from the Olive Barbet.

children.name: Stactolaema bouvieri
children.description: A barbet of Central African forests, with a green body and yellow throat.

children.name: Poagroenas
children.description: A small genus of East African barbets with distinctive black-and-white head markings and bright yellow underparts.

children.name: Poagroenas corvina
children.description: A barbet of the Ethiopian highlands, with a black cap, white face, and black bill. It inhabits montane forest and heathland.
